comment.body: "Equity Volatility: The VIX® index fell 1.8 pts last week, with therally in the SPX® index (+1.5%) accounting for 1.35 of the 1.8 ptdecrease. The rest of the VIX decline was mostly due to fixedstrike vols repricing lower, as the entire skew surface parallelshifted down. While the absolute level of implied volatility hasnormalized across regions, there is a sharp difference in thevolatility risk premium in the US vs. Europe and the rest of theworld. For example, while the 1M implied-realized spread isnegative for SPX/RTY/QQQ, the vol risk premium is positive andsignificant for SX5E/DAX and MXEA/MXEF. The divergence islargely a function of the higher realized vol in the US in recentweeks (SPX 1M realized vol of 16.7% vs. MXEF at 10.7%), whilethe options market is pricing for that to abate significantly (bothSPX and MXEF 1M implied vol trading ~13.5%). See Exhibit 2.Skew and Convexity: Skew flattened for short-dated optionslast week as investors used calls to chase the rally. This wasparticularly pronounced in small-caps. RTY 1M skew (25-deltaratio) fell from the 96th percentile high to the 45th percentile low.This was largely driven by higher demand for upside calls. RTY1M call skew (25D/50D ratio) surged from the 8th percentile lowto the 85th percentile high (see Exhibit 3) – highest since thepost-election euphoria last fall. However, while sentiment hasturned positive in the near-term, longer-term caution stillprevails, with longer-dated skew trading at elevated levelsacross indices. SPX 6M skew, for example, is in the 93rdpercentile high while RTY 6M skew is in the 86th percentile high."
comment.raw_body: "Equity Volatility: The VIX® index fell 1.8 pts last week, with therally in the SPX® index (+1.5%) accounting for 1.35 of the 1.8 ptdecrease. The rest of the VIX decline was mostly due to fixedstrike vols repricing lower, as the entire skew surface parallelshifted down. While the absolute level of implied volatility hasnormalized across regions, there is a sharp difference in thevolatility risk premium in the US vs. Europe and the rest of theworld. For example, while the 1M implied-realized spread isnegative for SPX/RTY/QQQ, the vol risk premium is positive andsignificant for SX5E/DAX and MXEA/MXEF. The divergence islargely a function of the higher realized vol in the US in recentweeks (SPX 1M realized vol of 16.7% vs. MXEF at 10.7%), whilethe options market is pricing for that to abate significantly (bothSPX and MXEF 1M implied vol trading ~13.5%). See Exhibit 2.Skew and Convexity: Skew flattened for short-dated optionslast week as investors used calls to chase the rally. This wasparticularly pronounced in small-caps. RTY 1M skew (25-deltaratio) fell from the 96th percentile high to the 45th percentile low.This was largely driven by higher demand for upside calls. RTY1M call skew (25D/50D ratio) surged from the 8th percentile lowto the 85th percentile high (see Exhibit 3) – highest since thepost-election euphoria last fall. However, while sentiment hasturned positive in the near-term, longer-term caution stillprevails, with longer-dated skew trading at elevated levelsacross indices. SPX 6M skew, for example, is in the 93rdpercentile high while RTY 6M skew is in the 86th percentile high."
